rows.notes: Header/title at top of list. Ink is fairly clear for the label but the small monetary figures at the extreme right (if any) are partly cut off by the page edge and shadow, so no clear amount is assigned.
Description legible. There is a small set of marks to the right but they are darkened/obscured by the gutter and shadow; no distinct three-column £ s d pattern can be reliably read for this row.

The line includes apparent internal numeric annotations (written inline with the description). There are no distinct columnar amounts clearly aligned to the three right-hand ruling lines for this row; the inline numbers are part of the description.
Description is legible. No clear separate £/s/d in the rightmost columns for this row (shaded area and faint ruling make any small figures ambiguous).

The description contains an inline numeric annotation (4.14.11) that appears part of the item text rather than clearly placed in the £/s/d columns. The right-hand monetary columns are not clearly aligned to that inline figure.
Single-word entry; no distinct columnar amounts visible to the right for this row.
Legible name. The right-hand area adjacent to this row is faint and shadowed, so no reliable amount is extracted.
Short name clearly readable. No separate £/s/d numerals are visible in proper columns beside it.

The word '10' is visible near the description (appears to be '10s' as part of the description). There are no clearly aligned £ s d columns showing this as a column value; treated as part of the description.
Handwritten inline figure '6:8' appears within the description. The columns to the extreme right are faint at this vertical position so I did not place a column amount.
The surname and an apparent note follow; the wording is partially abbreviated and slightly smudged. No distinct columnar £ s d visible here.
Clear short entry. No adjacent amount in the right-hand ledger columns at this row.
Readable entry; right-hand numeric columns have no distinct aligned figure here (shadow and faint ink).
Legible entry. No clear £/s/d immediately to the right of this row.
The inline annotation '2.7.5d' is part of the description; the right-side ledger columns are not clearly separated here and the inline notation may not map to column positions.
Inline numeric annotation present in the description. No clear column alignment to place these as £/s/d in the rightmost ruling lines.
Tintinhull appears as a place/header with two bracketed names (Rush and Hopkins). No amounts visible on the right tied to this header.
Readable place name; no aligned amounts in the right columns at this row.

The right-hand columns show a clear, well-aligned amount opposite this entry: '01 16 11' (interpreted as £1 16s 11d). Vertical alignment with ruling lines is consistent and values fall within valid ranges.
Label 'Brewhouse' is legible. Adjacent columns are faint here; no distinct £/s/d extracted.
Description clear; no clearly aligned amount in right-side ledger columns for this row.

rows.description: Cuddington Augm. Red. ad Fest. Purificacions
rows.notes: Long, abbreviated Latinized note in the description. The right-hand ledger area nearby is smudged/shadowed; cannot reliably read a three-column amount.
Text legible but the possible numbers to the right are partly obscured by a shadow; one faint cluster appears but pence columns are unclear, so left blank to avoid invalid pence/shilling values.
Follow-up line to previous entry. Right-hand numerals are faint and partly under a shadow; I could not confidently align an amount to the three ruling lines.
Entry text clear. No distinct columnar numbers visible immediately to the right.

rows.description: Ladlane, mr Sparks and Meriton Ann. 17.13.4
rows.notes: The description carries an inline numeric annotation '17.13.4' which appears part of the textual annotation rather than clearly separated into the £/s/d columns. Ink is faint here so column placement is uncertain.
Place/church name is readable though slightly abbreviated. Adjacent amounts are faint and not clearly aligned to the ruling lines.
Backgate appears as a header/section label with a curly brace indicating the following lines are sub-items. No amount written on this header line itself.

There is a numerically aligned cluster opposite this line. I read the group (in the three columns) as '00 06 13' based on vertical alignment with the ruling lines, but the pence value 13 is above the valid 0–11 range which suggests possible misalignment or a transcription of '6/13' as a ledger internal notation. Because pence > 11 is invalid, this reading is uncertain; included here with low confidence. (If interpreting the three right-most digits as '0 6 13' this fails currency validation — the pence digit is ambiguous, could be '1' or '3' or part of adjacent row. Proceed with caution.)
There is a clear cluster opposite this line read as '00 04 00' aligned with the three ruling lines. The alignment looks consistent and the values lie within valid ranges.

Boxed total at right ('125 10 10') is very clearly written and aligned in the three-column format (pounds 125, shillings 10, pence 10). High confidence: vertical lines and the boxed presentation make this unambiguous.

rows.description: Recd of mr. Verman for Batts due to ye Coll.
rows.notes: Right-side numerals opposite this receipt are clearly '13 17 10' and align with the £ s d columns. Ink is slightly uneven but digits are legible and within valid currency ranges.

rows.description: and for so much pd to mr Berry ye Brewer
rows.notes: This line appears to be a continuation/explanatory line related to the previous receipt. There are faint small marks to the right but no clearly separate £ s d aligned amount; treated as descriptive continuation.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins Bursar for Increments
rows.notes: Text legible but the monetary cluster to the right (if present) is faint and partly obscured by shadow; cannot place amounts confidently into columns for this row.

A group of names follows (mr Vincent, mr Treby, mr Davie etc.) and a clear aligned amount '40 00 00' is visible on the right associated with this cluster. The context suggests this is the total received for the 'Gent Commoners'. The handwriting of the bracketed names is faint; amount alignment is better than the text, but because multiple names share it I mark confidence moderate.

rows.description: mr Vincent 8t mr Treby 8t mr Davie 8t
rows.notes: These are individual names/allocations listed under the previous 'Gent Commoners' receipt. The amounts for each individual are not separated into distinct aligned £/s/d columns on the page; the main '40 00 00' appears to be a grouped total, so individual monetary amounts are not extracted here. Handwriting is somewhat smudged.

There is a smaller aligned amount '04 00 00' visible near these names, but the ink is lighter and the grouping ambiguous (could be part of the previous grouped receipts). I assign it cautiously; validation is satisfied but context is uncertain.

rows.description: ms for Act supper; and mr Lane 2t mr Marker 2t
rows.notes: A small aligned figure '00 04 00' appears adjacent to this line. Ink faint and grouping ambiguous; the description contains many abbreviations and the numeric placement is not crisp, so confidence is moderate-low.

rows.description: Ballts for Egg Saturday and Ashwednesday
rows.notes: Description legible though abbreviated. Any monetary marks to the right are faint/dashed and not clearly legible into the three-column format.

rows.description: St Potbury 2t St Orchard 2t St Gee 2t St Pennington 2t
rows.notes: A list of parishes/places with small annotations '2t' which appear as part of the text. There are faint numerals to the extreme right but they do not align cleanly to £/s/d columns for distinct extraction.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for ye Common Library
rows.notes: At the lower right there is a cluster '27 7 ?' that appears tied to a 'Recd of mr Hutchins' line. The pence digit is partly cut off/faint; pounds=27 and shillings=7 are relatively clear, pence unreadable, so pence left blank. Because of partial visibility confidence is moderate-low.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for money for 2 year July 16th 1701
rows.notes: This continuation/related receipt line appears at the bottom of the page. There are faint totals near the margin but the shadow and curvature of the page make the exact three-column reading ambiguous, so amounts are left blank here.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for ye Common Library (final line)
rows.notes: At the bottom-right margin there is a small grouped figure that looks like '115 00 00' but the ink is very faint at the extreme corner and the page is curved; pence/shillings appear zero if that is the case. Because this is at the page edge and partly cut off, confidence is low (<0.5).
